the fraction $\frac{100!}{10^{50}}$ is written as an irreducible fraction, meaning that the numerator and denominator have no common factors other than 1. to find the denominator of this fraction, we need to determine the prime factors of the numerator and the denominator, and then simplify the fraction by dividing out any common factors. the denominator of the irreducible fraction will be the product of the prime factors of the numerator that are not canceled out by the denominator.
